title: Urine on Carpet
stainChemistry: Fresh urine is an acidic solution (pH 5-6) containing urea, uric acid, creatinine, ammonia precursors, and trace proteins. As urine dries, bacteria break down urea into ammonia, shifting the pH alkaline (8-9+) and creating the characteristic odor. Uric acid crystals form and bond to carpet fibers — these crystals are extremely difficult to remove because they are insoluble in water and re-activate with moisture, causing phantom odors to return even after cleaning. The protein components can also set into the carpet backing and pad under the alkaline conditions of aging urine.
whyThisWorks: Enzyme cleaners containing urease (breaks uric acid crystals) and Protein Formula (addresses protein components) are the only truly effective treatment for urine. Standard cleaners mask the odor but leave the uric acid crystals intact — which reactivate with humidity. Complete dwell time and extraction is critical. The enzymatic reaction must be allowed to complete fully before the area is dried.
defaultAssumption: Treat as possibly dried with uric acid crystal formation. Even if the area looks dry and odor-free, uric acid crystals remain active and will re-odor with moisture.

steps: 1. Blot fresh urine thoroughly — do not rub
2. Extract as much liquid as possible — wet-vac preferred
3. Rinse with cold water — extract again
4. Apply Protein Formula (Biokleen Bac-Out, Bridgepoint Urine Destroyer, or equivalent) generously — saturate to reach pad if possible
5. Cover with plastic wrap to prevent drying — allow minimum 24-hour dwell
7. Apply fresh enzyme solution for second pass — dwell another hour
8. Final cold water rinse and extraction
9. Fan dry completely — do not cover until fully dry
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — never use hot water (sets protein) or steam before Protein Formula
warnings: NEVER use steam before Protein Formula — sets protein permanently
NEVER use ammonia-based cleaners — matches urine pH and does not remove odor compounds
Thorough extraction is critical — leaving enzyme solution in carpet can cause browning
Large or old stains may have penetrated pad — pad replacement may be necessary for full odor elimination

steps: 1. Blot immediately with clean cloths — extract as much as possible
2. Pour cold water over area — blot again to dilute
3. Apply enzyme pet cleaner (Nature's Miracle, Rocco & Roxie) — saturate area
4. Cover with plastic wrap — dwell minimum 8-24 hours
6. Baking soda on dried area — vacuum after 30 minutes for residual odor
diyProtocol.whenToStop: If odor returns with humidity after treatment, the uric acid crystals have not been fully broken down — professional extraction and fresh enzyme application is needed. If pad is soaked through, pad replacement may be the only solution.
warnings: Do not use bleach — does not remove uric acid
Do not use ammonia cleaners — makes it worse
Grocery store carpet cleaners (Resolve, etc.) mask odor but don't remove uric acid crystals
Do not cover area until completely dry — traps moisture and causes mold

neverDo: Ammonia-based cleaners — matches urine odor compounds
fiberSensitivities: Wool carpet: enzyme cleaners must be urease-based (not Protein Formula-heavy) — brief contact only
All carpets: extraction quality determines final outcome — wet-vac significantly better than blotting alone

escalation.when: Odor returns with humidity after multiple treatments, stain covers large area, or pad is soaked through
escalation.whatToTell: Urine stain — possibly deep in pad. Ask for enzyme injection into pad if available, or pad replacement in affected area. No steam before enzyme.
escalation.specialistType: IICRC-certified water damage or carpet cleaning specialist

contextQuestions: Is odor present when area is dry?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Fresh urine: excellent enzyme results. Old dried urine with uric acid crystals: 24-hour enzyme dwell essential. Pet urine vs human: similar treatment, pet urine often more concentrated. Odor when dry = uric acid crystals still present, need fresh enzyme pass. Pad soaked through: professional or pad replacement.
